intro: Established in 1918 to replace the Office of the Coroner, the Office of Chief Medical Examiner conducts independent forensic investigations into unexplained, violent, and suspicious deaths across the five boroughs. With about 860 employees, OCME investigates roughly 40,000 deaths a year and operates the largest public DNA crime laboratory in the world, making it the largest medical examiner's office in the country.

cardHighlights.function: Investigates unexplained, violent, and suspicious deaths citywide
